offer.what: Sheet music books, Gregorian chant guides, MIDI organ files, and the PTCO series (Practical Tools for Church Organists) for Rodgers/Roland digital organs — available as free downloads, Amazon paperbacks, and Kindle editions. Also a 14-week Free Chant School video curriculum.
offer.coreOutcome: Catholic church organists and choir directors can confidently lead liturgical music — reading Gregorian chant, registering a digital organ, and building a parish music library — without expensive materials or specialized training.
offer.pricePoint: Mostly free (download + donation requested); physical books on Amazon ~$10–$25; Kindle editions ~$5–$10. No subscription.
offer.commitment: One-off purchase or free download; no recurring contract.
leadMagnets: Free Chant School (14-week video curriculum for Gregorian chant)
Free Downloads page (PDFs of organ and choral books)
Free Music Shelf (curated free sheet music)
Free sample pages for PTCO organ series

audience.icp: Amateur or semi-professional Catholic church organist (often a volunteer or part-time parish musician) who plays a Rodgers or Roland digital organ and needs accessible, liturgically correct music at little or no cost.
painPoints: Gregorian chant notation is unfamiliar and intimidating
Transitioning from piano/keyboard to pipe or digital organ
Parish music budgets are extremely tight — free materials are essential
Hard to find Catholic-specific (not generic Protestant) liturgical repertoire
Isolated role with few peers — limited professional development resources
segments: Parish organists (Rodgers/Roland digital organ owners — PTCO series audience)
Choir directors in Catholic parishes seeking choral & chant repertoire
Gregorian chant learners / beginners (chant school audience)

salesCycle.length: Same day — free download or one-click Amazon purchase; no approval or consultation needed.
salesCycle.trial: Yes — essentially everything is free to start. The free download library and Free Chant School serve as a zero-friction trial of the full catalog.
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Self-serve: visitor downloads a free PDF or buys on Amazon. Donation page nudges free users to contribute. No salesperson.
salesCycle.topObjection: "I can get this for free, so why donate or buy the print edition?" — the free model undercuts its own upsell. Secondary objection: "Is this quality/authoritative enough for our parish?"

ahaMoment: The moment a musician successfully sings or plays through a Gregorian chant piece (or organ hymn) using the free resources and realizes they can now lead their choir or congregation — typically after completing the first few lessons of the Free Chant School or playing through one of the PTCO organ books.

retention.recurring: No formal subscription. Retention is community- and mission-driven: continued downloads of new publications, engagement with The Catholic Organist's Quarterly volumes, and ongoing reference use of the MIDI library.
retention.expansion: Buying additional seasonal volumes of The Catholic Organist's Quarterly (multiple volumes: Fall, Winter, Spring, Summer, Manuals Only editions), purchasing other books in the chant or organ series, or donating after sustained free use.
retention.churnRisk: Silent disengagement — free users download once and never return. No subscription means no renewal trigger. New publication releases are the main re-engagement hook.

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding: No apparent nurture sequence for new free-download subscribers; people receive a PDF and disappear.
Lead-Magnet Nurture: Free Chant School generates leads but likely no automated email sequence guiding learners through the curriculum and into paid books.
Value Newsletter: Content exists (quarterly volumes, MIDI library updates) but likely not distributed via a consistent email newsletter.
Renewal/Anniversary/Expansion: No trigger to promote new seasonal volumes to past buyers or re-engage dormant downloaders.
Win-Back/Re-Engagement: Likely entirely absent — no mechanism to re-activate subscribers who stopped opening emails.
Referral/Advocacy: No structured referral or parish-share program despite strong word-of-mouth potential among Catholic musicians.
toneVoice: Warm, scholarly, and mission-driven. Educational without being condescending — Noel Jones writes for beginners but holds an AAGO credential, so the voice balances accessibility with quiet authority. Devotional undertone (sacred music as ministry). Not commercial; price is almost never the lead. Think: a trusted colleague in the choir loft, not a marketer.
compliance: CAN-SPAM / CASL standard email compliance. Religious/faith-based content — no HIPAA, financial, or legal compliance requirements. Should be sensitive to the pastoral context (volunteers, small parishes, clergy) and avoid aggressive sales language.
